authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 Alpine Frog Lake Rainbow Trout Fingerling Put and Grow August

sources.title: Frog Lake, Winnemucca Lake, Round Top Lake, and Fourth of July Lake, Alpine County — CDFW Region 2 Fisheries
sources.quotedText: California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) historically stocked Frog Lake with fingerling-sized Brook Trout (Salvelinus fontinalis; BK), Rainbow Trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss; RT), and Lahontan Cutthroat Trout (Oncorhynchus clarki henshawi; LCT) with the last recorded stocking of LCT in August 2020. Prior to stocking in 2020, the most recent stocking at Frog Lake was an allotment of RT in 2018. CDFW currently manages Frog Lake as a “put and grow” fishery. Frog Lake is open all year for fishing and other recreational opportunities, but has limited access during the winter season when there is snow.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 260425, "Frog Lake", Lake, Alpine County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":260425,"gaz_name":"Frog Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Alpine"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13356778.3167, 4677049.9975) converts to lon -119.985981, lat 38.687802, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-119.9860, 38.6879) to within ~15m. Note: a second, distinct Frog Lake (gaz_id 260424, also Alpine County) exists ~15.5km south at approximately -120.0035, 38.5477 — not to be confused with this one.

parameters.note: Frog Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Frog Lake (Region 2, Alpine Co.) for fingerling-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month of August. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
